title: schrankia hystricina
intro: Schrankia hystricina, also known as the hairy indigo plant, is a type of flowering plant in the legume family that is native to Australia and Southeast Asia. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of the plant have been used in herbal medicine, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Schrankia hystricina include its ability to reduce swelling, relieve pain, and treat wounds, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve circulation. Herbal preparations of Schrankia hystricina include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from minor skin irritations to more serious conditions such as eczema and acne.
